description_long: This object is a rotary outdoor clothesline, commonly known as a rotary dryer or umbrella clothesline. It features a central vertical metal pole supporting a rotating frame with multiple arms that extend outward, each strung with parallel lines for hanging laundry. The arms are connected by taut cords, creating a square or rectangular drying area, and the entire structure is elevated to allow garments to hang freely. The base pole is designed to be inserted into the ground or a mounting socket for stability. The frame is primarily metallic, with some plastic or composite connectors at the joints, and the lines are likely made of durable synthetic fiber. A blue rectangular board with mattress size markings is visible in some views, likely for scale or as a background element.
description: A rotary outdoor clothesline with a central metal pole and multiple arms strung with drying lines, forming a square drying area. The structure is metallic with taut cords and a blue background board for scale.
description_view_0: Front view showing the rotary clothesline with all arms and lines extended, and a blue board with mattress size markings behind it.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thin profile of the arms and lines, with the blue board visible to the right.
description_view_2: Top-down view showing the square arrangement of the drying lines and the full surface of the blue board beneath.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with the blue board now on the left, showing the symmetry of the arms and lines.
